name: Frashokereti
shortDescription: Frashokereti is the Zoroastrian doctrine of the final renovation of the universe, when evil will be destroyed, and everything will be made perfect.
description: Frashokereti, also spelled Frashkart, is a central eschatological doctrine in Zoroastrianism, representing the final renovation of the cosmos. It signifies the ultimate triumph of good over evil, the resurrection of the dead, and the establishment of a perfect world free from suffering, decay, and death. This transformative event is brought about through the actions of a savior figure, the Saoshyant, and the collective efforts of humanity to uphold Asha (truth and righteousness) against Druj (falsehood and deceit). The process involves the reintegration of all creation into a state of wholeness and perfection, mirroring the original, ideal state envisioned by Ahura Mazda. The final battle against evil culminates in the annihilation of Angra Mainyu (Ahriman), the embodiment of destructive forces. Frashokereti is not merely a passive event but an active process in which humans play a crucial role through their ethical choices and righteous deeds. It represents the culmination of Zoroastrian ethics and the ultimate goal of human existence: to cooperate with Ahura Mazda in defeating evil and restoring the world to its original perfection. The concept shares similarities with eschatological beliefs in other religions, such as the Christian concept of the Second Coming and the establishment of God's kingdom on Earth. It also bears resemblance to cyclical worldviews found in other mythologies, where periods of cosmic order and chaos alternate, eventually leading to a renewed and purified world. Frashokereti is a powerful symbol of hope and the potential for cosmic redemption, emphasizing the importance of individual responsibility in the ongoing struggle between good and evil.

sources.title: The Wiley Blackwell Companion to Zoroastrianism
sources.author: Michael Stausberg and Yuhan Sohrab-Dinshaw Vevaina (Editors)
